projectDescription: A Spring Boot REST API for managing First Lego League competition logistics, including teams, matches, scoring, leaderboards, and volunteer coordination.
keyFeatures: Edition lifecycle management (DRAFT → OPEN → CLOSED) with state transition validation
Team registration and management with max-team enforcement (18 teams per edition)
Match scheduling and assignment with uniqueness constraint (no two teams play each other twice)
Real-time score registration and automatic ranking/leaderboard calculation
Volunteer role management with conflict prevention (Judge/Referee role validation)
Project room management with judge assignment (manager + panelists model)
HAL+JSON hypermedia API with Spring Data REST and HATEOAS
Cucumber BDD integration tests covering all major scenarios
Stateless HTTP Basic authentication with Spring Security
publicApiSurface: GET /identity — Get current authenticated user identity
POST /editions/{editionId}/teams/{teamId} — Register a team to an edition
GET /editions/{editionId}/volunteers — Get volunteers grouped by type
GET /editions/{editionId}/tables — Get competition tables for an edition
GET /leaderboards/editions/{editionId} — Get paginated leaderboard for an edition
GET /matches/search/findByTeam — Find matches by team URI
POST /matches/{matchId}/assign-table — Assign a competition table to a match
POST /matchAssignments/assign — Assign a referee to a match
POST /matchAssignments/batch — Batch assign referees to matches in a round
POST /matchResults/register — Register final match score and recalculate rankings
POST /project-rooms/assign-judge — Assign a judge to a project room
POST /teams/assign-coach — Assign a coach to a team
